element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Interactive level exploration and resource discovery
High-damage area weapon against tougher foes
Temporary speed and melee power boost

typical_relief_triggers: Destruction of destructible props for resources
typical_tension_triggers: Encounter with Pig Cops and mutated enemies
Narrow corridors and air ducts with enemies
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced arcade action with intensity peaks at enemy encounters, modulated by interactive environmental exploration and non-linear routing options that provide tactical breathing room

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Destructible objects hint at secret or alternative passages; water fountains and health pickups cluster in transitional spaces
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Air ducts, back doors, sewers serve as hidden routes; interactive props (strippers, fire hydrants) signal environmental interactivity and potential secrets

difficulty_curve.early_game: Piece of Cake difficulty emphasizes introduction to mechanics with basic enemy types (Pig Cops, lesser mutants) and fundamental weapons (pistol, shotgun)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation of enemy density and introduction of specialized foes; expansion of arsenal to include RPG, chain gun, freezethrower; complex level layouts requiring jetpack and environmental mastery
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters, advanced enemy combinations, resource scarcity management, mastery of shrink ray and exotic weapons
notable_spikes: Episode transitions introduce new enemy types and environmental hazards
Boss-heavy levels (E3L11: Freeway, E4L3: Shop-N-Bag noted in speedrun times suggest difficulty variance)
Late episode enemy density and projectile patterns

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels encourage players to interact with and destroy props to discover health, ammo caches, and secret passages. Most destructible objects reward curiosity with gameplay benefits.
Multiple paths through levels via air ducts, back doors, and sewers allow players to avoid direct enemy engagement or access weapons before frontal confrontation
Each level (or episode climax) typically concludes with a significant boss encounter that requires mastery of accumulated weapons and tactics
Environmental objects like strippers, light switches, and fire hydrants serve dual purpose: gameplay benefits and environmental flavor/satire
